# Disk Cleanup Candidate Identification — 200 GiB Target (v2)

**Generated by:** `/swarm` run (ultracode Workflow tool), 8 collect lanes
(worldarchitect-decompose, worktree-full-sweep, library-decompose,
gemini-decompose, project-dedup-verify [incl. PAT-rotation security check],
ao-hermes-codex, dirs-cleaner-retry, review-tier-refresh) → extract → 3-lens
adversarial verify → synthesis. Workflow run ID `wf_5aaa388d-23e`
(task `wmy5yo616`). Generated 2026-07-18T08:13Z.

**Supersedes:** `roadmap/2026-07-17-plan-700g-target.md`. That prior plan's
SAFE tier (~94.56 GiB) plus the Colima `ez-gh-actions` fstrim fix were
already **executed** (see that doc's Execution log). Its REVIEW tier
(~98.04 GiB) is still pending explicit approval and is being re-verified
and re-decomposed in this pass.

**Baseline:** the prior plan cited 731.3 GiB live usage (measured at
mission start via `df -kP /System/Volumes/Data`) against an 821.2 GiB
pre-cleanup baseline, a 700 GiB floor, and this pass's user-requested
**200 GiB cleanup-candidate identification target**. Re-measuring live
just now (`df -kP /System/Volumes/Data`) shows **719.8 GiB** — lower than
the 731.3 GiB cited at mission start, consistent with incremental cleanup
and normal churn since; **719.8 GiB is the authoritative current figure**,
731.3 GiB is retained above only as the mission-start reference point.

---

## Security status

**Verdict: rotated / no longer exposed for the 3 originally-flagged repos.**
Direct inspection just now (`grep` on `.git/config` remote URLs, redacting
any match) for all three repos named in the prior plan's finding —
`~/worldarchitect.ai`, `~/projects_reference/jleechanclaw`,
`~/repos/jleechanclaw` — found:

- `~/worldarchitect.ai/.git/config` → plain `https://github.com/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai.git`, **no embedded token**.
- `~/projects_reference/jleechanclaw` → **directory no longer exists at that path** (jleechanclaw was relocated to `~/project_jleechanclaw/jleechanclaw` and `~/jleechanclaw`, both checked and also clean — plain `https://github.com/jleechanorg/jleechanclaw.git`).
- `~/repos/jleechanclaw/.git/config` → plain `https://github.com/jleechanorg/jleechanclaw.git`, **no embedded token**.

None of the three originally-flagged remote URLs still carry an embedded
PAT. This corroborates (does not merely repeat) the "rotate immediately"
action item from issue #27 having actually been carried out.

**New finding this pass (not part of the original 3):** a machine-wide
`grep` for `ghp_*`/`github_pat_*` patterns embedded in `.git/config` remote
URLs turned up **two new, currently-live-looking exposures**, both scoped
to the `jleechan2015` GitHub account and both pointing at `gist.github.com`:

- `/private/tmp/gist-evidence-clone/.git/config`
- `/private/tmp/gist_wc_c1ch_evidence/.git/config`

Both are scratch clones under `/private/tmp` used for gist-based evidence
uploads (per this repo's evidence-standards workflow). **Action needed:**
rotate/revoke these two tokens and confirm the `/private/tmp` scratch
clones are deleted after use — do not assume `/private/tmp` is
self-cleaning. This is a new, separate finding from the original 3 and is
tracked as its own follow-up (see Provenance section).

---

## Ranked candidate table

**HARD REQUIREMENT check:** every row below is ≤ 5 GiB. The single largest
row (`~/project_worldaiclaw/worldai_claw/packages/mobile/ios/build`,
5.0 GiB) sits exactly at the ceiling and needed no further decomposition
because direct inspection (SAFE-tier votes) confirmed its full 5.0 GiB is
already a single atomic Xcode `DerivedData` + codegen output tree with no
further meaningful subdivision (`DerivedData` alone accounts for ~4.96 GiB
of it; `generated/` is a trivial 764 KB) — decomposing further would not
change the action (delete via `xcodebuild clean`/rebuild) or the byte
count. No row exceeds 5 GiB; none required forced decomposition beyond
what the source lanes already did.

**De-duplication note:** two SAFE candidates that were literally the same
bytes measured at two nesting depths (`.../ios/build/DerivedData` and the
parent `.../ios/build`) were merged into one row (`ios/build`, 5.0 GiB) to
avoid double-counting. Two REVIEW candidates (`~/repos/jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai`
whole-clone and `~/project_agento/worktree_worldarchitect` whole-worktree)
are **known supersets of their own `venv`/`.venv` sub-rows** already listed
separately in this table — they are shown for context but **excluded from
the running total** to avoid double-counting the same physical bytes twice.

## Running total toward 200 GiB — honest gap disclosure

**De-duplicated grand total identified this pass: 53.24 GiB** (SAFE 16.65
GiB + REVIEW 36.59 GiB additive). This is well short of the 200 GiB
identification target. Per the "verify before reporting" rule, I am not
papering over this gap:

- **What's covered:** essentially all of the AO/Antigravity/Codex worktree
  and venv sprawl, browser/app caches, Godot/Discord/Zoom/TikTok/Cursor
  redownloadable app data, and a first pass at the `.gemini`/`.hermes`
  bloat already flagged in prior docs.
- **What's NOT yet in this 53.24 GiB and still needs a dedicated pass:**
  - The prior plan's **REVIEW tier (~98.04 GiB)** from
    `2026-07-17-plan-700g-target.md` overlaps partially but was not
    re-summed 1:1 into this table — several of its named buckets (the
    41 GiB worldarchitect.ai `.claude/worktrees` sweep, 32.98 GiB of
    python venvs) are represented here only via their *individually
    re-verified* sub-candidates, which is why this pass's total is lower,
    not higher, than the prior estimate — the discrepancy is decomposition
    granularity, not missing space.
  - `/private/var/dirs_cleaner` (~165 GiB per the bead's dirs-cleaner-retry
    lane target) — **direct `du`/`exec` measurement is blocked by macOS
    Launch Constraints** in this session; retry via an alternate
    measurement method (e.g. `mdls`/Storage Management API, or a
    privileged helper) is still open work, not resolved this pass.
  - Colima sparse-disk growth and the ~213 GiB unattributed
    accounting-gap named in prior memory
    (`project_2026-07-15_disk_swing_mechanisms_confirmed.md`) — TCC/SIP
    paths and MobileSync are the leading suspects, not measured here.
  - `~/Library/Application Support`/`~/Library/Messages` were only
    one-level-deep decomposed in the prior pass
    (`roadmap/evidence/library_full_breakdown_20260717.tsv`); this pass
    added several new named sub-buckets (Godot, Discord, Zoom, TikTok,
    Cursor, Conductor, Warp, Fellow, Aside, Atlas) but has not
    exhaustively walked every remaining `Application Support/*` subfolder.

**Conclusion:** this pass materially advances decomposition quality
(everything ≤5 GiB, adversarially verified, de-duplicated) but does **not**
by itself reach the 200 GiB target. Closing the gap requires either (a) an
alternate measurement path for `/private/var/dirs_cleaner`, or (b) explicit
user sign-off to execute the SAFE tier now (16.65 GiB) and re-baseline
before continuing REVIEW-tier decomposition against the smaller residual.

---

## Do not touch / never-delete

The following are explicitly protected and were **excluded from every
lane's candidate list** in this pass:

- `~/.codex/sessions*` (glob — includes `sessions/` and `sessions_archive/`, ~19 GiB + 1.9 GiB)
- `~/.codex/state*.sqlite`
- `~/.codex/log`
- `~/.claude/projects`
- `~/.local/orch-venv` — confirmed **live-referenced** by a currently-running AO session's `PATH`; do not remove.
- `~/.hermes/state.db` — active production database backing the running Hermes gateway.

---

## Refuted / rejected

No candidate in this pass was net-rejected by the 3-lens adversarial vote
(every row above carried a majority "survives" verdict). However, several
candidates drew at least one dissenting/refuting vote for reasons future
passes should not silently re-litigate:

- **`~/.gemini/antigravity-cli` "explained portion" (2.32 GiB)** — refuted
  by one lane as a path/size-category mismatch: the *whole* directory is
  23 GiB, and this figure only ever explained 2 specific files. Superseded
  in this report by the two individual, directly-measured task-log rows
  in the SAFE tier (`task-618.log` 1.4 GiB, `task-224.log` 0.92 GiB).
- **`~/Library/Application Support/Google/DriveFS/<account-UUID>`** —
  refuted: deleting `metadata_sqlite_db` would break the currently
  populated, browsable "My Drive" mount (1,435 real user folders);
  requires quitting/disabling Google Drive first, not a routine cache
  clear. Kept at REVIEW, not upgraded to SAFE.
- **`~/Library/Application Support/Google/DriveFS/Resources`** — refuted:
  contents are live app-bundle locale (`.lproj`) resources, not a cache;
  it was grouped into the DriveFS "safe-ish" bucket by directory proximity
  only, not by content inspection.
- **`~/Library/Caches/com.steipete.codexbar`** — refuted: a live Sparkle
  auto-updater process (`Updater`, PID observed executing from inside this
  exact path) was mid-update at check time; do not delete while an update
  is in flight. Re-check liveness immediately before any action.
- **`~/Library/Caches/CodexBar`** — refuted: contents are a locally
  computed derived index (rescans `~/.codex` and `~/.claude/projects`),
  not network-redownloadable; "regenerable" is true but costs a full local
  rescan, not a quick re-fetch.
- **`~/worldarchitect.ai.worktrees/pr7558-fix`** and
  **`~/repos/jleechanorg/worktree_scroll_slow`** — the stated
  "unpushed-ahead" reasoning was refuted as factually backwards in both
  cases (both branches are actually fully merged/superseded upstream,
  zero unique commits) — the underlying SAFE-to-remove conclusion holds
  or is even stronger, but the *reasoning field* needs correction before
  reuse.
- **Several `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` and `jleechanclaw*` paths**
  (`agent-ad0361851ebd587e0`, `agent-a3b4f298a8d8eb845`,
  `fix-bd-gth5-action-fix`, `jleechanclaw-ao-plumbing-sync-20260312-204713`)
  — one voter in each case could not locate the path from the wrong
  cwd/repo-root and refuted on "path doesn't exist"; subsequent voters
  resolved the repo-root ambiguity and confirmed the path is real. Not
  rejected, but a reminder to always resolve absolute paths against the
  correct repo root before scripting deletion (same class of mistake as
  memory `feedback_2026-07-17_br_beads_scoped_by_cwd_not_global.md`).
- **`~/.worktrees/jleechanclaw/fix-bd-gth5-action-fix`** — one vote found
  real, uncommitted, unpushed script diffs (`scripts/backup_cron_jobs.sh`
  and edits to `monitor-agent.sh`/`doctor.sh`/`simplify-daily.sh`) inside
  this otherwise-orphaned worktree; recommend extracting those diffs
  before deletion, not blind removal.

## Execution log — 2026-07-18 (SAFE tier, conservative subset)

Executed the SAFE tier per explicit user approval, with two conservative
exclusions the user asked for after reviewing the list:

- **Excluded the 4 Antigravity `task-NNN.log` files (task-618, task-224,
  task-16, task-42; 2.59 GiB combined)** — on inspection these are raw
  per-task execution traces (system prompts + embedded tool-output/grep
  dumps), not curated conversation transcripts; the user explicitly wants
  to retain LLM conversation history and a separate curated archive
  already exists at `~/Library/CloudStorage/Dropbox/conversation-backups/antigravity/`
  (2,815 markdown exports). Left untouched rather than assert zero risk.

**Real discrepancy found during independent pre-delete verification (filed
as bead `jleechan-6mu5`):** the `~/.ao/data/worktrees/{7 auto-export stubs}`
row claimed 0.00002 GiB total, SAFE. Direct check before touching found
this badly wrong — `user-scope-auto-export-5` alone is 1.6 GiB,
`llm-wiki-auto-export-1` has dozens of uncommitted `D` deletions of
`.beads/.br_history/*.jsonl` files, `llm-wiki-auto-export-6` has 63,845
lines of uncommitted `git status --short` output, and
`delegated-auto-export-1` points at a dangling worktree gitdir under a
stale `/private/var/folders/.../test_auto_push.*` tmp path. **This entire
category was skipped — nothing in `.ao/data/worktrees/*-auto-export*` was
touched.**

**Also caught and resolved:** three `.codex/worktrees/*/worldarchitect.ai`
rows showed enormous (9330-9339) "commits ahead" counts on a naive
`git rev-list` check — a false signal from this repo's rewritten/
squash-merged main-branch history, not real unpushed work. Verified via
`gh api repos/.../pulls/<n>` that the exact HEAD commit of each (PRs
#7155, #7237, #7208) is merged before removing.

**What was actually executed** (verified via `df -kP /System/Volumes/Data`
before/after): **15.17 GiB reclaimed** (731.75 → 716.58 GiB used,
145.17 → 160.33 GiB free) —
- iOS `build`/`Pods` for `worldai_claw` (5.29 GiB) — no live process had
  the build dir open despite a 5+ hour running Simulator instance of the
  app (it uses its own copied bundle, not the source `ios/build` tree);
  `Podfile.lock` confirmed `Pods` is regenerable.
- Messages `Caches/Previews` + `Caches/Plugins` (1.5 GiB).
- Godot export templates, whole directory, confirmed matching the
  report's itemized sum exactly (1.9 GiB); no Godot process running.
- Warp autoupdate cache, Discord `download`+`Cache`, DriveFS `Logs`+
  `cef_cache`, TikTok LIVE Studio `gecko_cache`/`effect`/`transition`,
  Aside/Chrome `*_crx_cache` dirs, Chrome `OptGuideOnDeviceClassifierModel`
  (~1.8 GiB combined) — verified no open file handles; Discord/TikTok
  confirmed not running.
- `.codex/.tmp/plugins` (77M).
- `worldarchitect.ai` git `tmp_pack_*` orphaned pack files (11 files,
  0.45 GiB) — confirmed `gc.pid` referenced a dead PID (stale lock, not
  an in-progress gc) before removing.
- `.git/lost-found` in `worldarchitect.ai` (23M).
- `~/.hermes/.git` — ran `git gc --prune=now` (non-destructive repack,
  no reachable commit touched) instead of hand-removing garbage files;
  reclaimed far more than the report's 0.024 GiB estimate — packs
  consolidated 21→1, size-pack 2.36 GiB → 1.09 GiB (~1.27 GiB bonus from
  proper repacking, not just garbage removal).
- `~/.hermes/logs/mem0_server.error.log` — truncated in place (`: >
  file`), not deleted, since it's an actively-written log.
- 5 `worldarchitect.ai` `.claude/worktrees/agent-*`/`wf_*` entries —
  independently re-verified clean + zero-ahead-of-origin/main immediately
  before removal via `git worktree remove --force` (the fresh
  `worktree_hygiene.sh` dry-run this pass only surfaced 1 of the 5 before
  hitting its own scan window; the other 4 were verified directly).
- 3 `.codex/worktrees/*/worldarchitect.ai` entries (see PR-verification
  note above), removed + `git worktree prune`'d from the main repo.
- 4 standalone orphaned worktree directories with dead gitdir references
  (`project_agento/worktree_antigravity_fix`,
  `projects_reference/jleechanclaw-ao-plumbing-sync-20260312-204713`,
  `project_jleechanclaw/jleechanclaw-monitor-agent-only`,
  `project_jleechanclaw/jleechanclaw_fresh_main`) — each confirmed
  git-orphaned (referenced worktree metadata no longer exists in any
  live main repo) and genuinely stale (oldest file mtimes, not recent
  activity — a misleading recent `.beads/beads.db*` mtime on all three
  turned out to be the systemic `ai.hermes.schedule.beads-conflict-resolver`
  launchd job, not unique work).
- 1 Antigravity `brain/*/worktrees/subagent-PR-8132-*` entry (311M) —
  verified PR #8132 merged before removing; plus 1 empty
  `brain/*/worktrees/` directory.
